user_prompt: add a web server handler for "/reset_memory", and the javascript function `passreset_onsubmit` will be the one to submit information to it via ajax
this only works under security-level 0, the backend rejects this under any other security-level
agent_reply: - Security level `0`: shuts Bluetooth down, erases Bluedroid bonds from `bt_config.conf`, erases `bt_hosts`, erases `wifi_cfg`, then clears the live `BtHostList` and `WifiManager`.
- Added `memreset_onsubmit()` in [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:1284), posting to `/reset_memory` via AJAX.
- Added a status/error row under the Memory Reset button in [index.html](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html:341).
I kept `passreset_onsubmit()` on `/reset_password`; the Memory Reset panel was already wired to `memreset_onsubmit()`.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` and `pio run -e thefly_sec1` compile the changed files, then still fail at final link with the existing IRAM overflow: `iram0_0_seg` overflowed by `2152 bytes`.
- Security level `0`: shuts Bluetooth down, erases Bluedroid bonds from `bt_config.conf`, erases `bt_hosts`, erases `wifi_cfg`, then clears the live `BtHostList` and `WifiManager`.
- Added `memreset_onsubmit()` in [script.js](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/script.js:1284), posting to `/reset_memory` via AJAX.
- Added a status/error row under the Memory Reset button in [index.html](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/html/index.html:341).
I kept `passreset_onsubmit()` on `/reset_password`; the Memory Reset panel was already wired to `memreset_onsubmit()`.
- `pio run -e thefly_sec0` and `pio run -e thefly_sec1` compile the changed files, then still fail at final link with the existing IRAM overflow: `iram0_0_seg` overflowed by `2152 bytes`.
